subject: skl_foundation-persona-src-https-github-com-product-on-80fcbbb2-e15e5-skills-foundation-persona-skill-md_80fcbbb2
evidence: Persona analysis: 'A product team has completed a round of user interviews... and needs a persona to anchor an upcoming PRD and stakeholder presentation. You load this skill in product mode, feed it the interview synthesis, and it fills the template with evidence-linked claims.' Journey map analysis: 'The skill will reject five specific inadequate inputs: no persona or scope' and 'You have a completed interview synthesis from 12 users, survey data from 200 respondents, and a defined persona.' → chains_with, weight 0.85: persona produces the defined persona artifact that journey map explicitly requires as a prerequisite input. Without a persona, journey map refuses or downgrades to hypothesis mode.

subject: skl_discover-stakeholder-summary-src-https-github-com-e568212c-ls-discover-stakeholder-summary-skill-md_e568212c
evidence: Stakeholder summary analysis: 'plan communication' (step 6 of 7), 'plan communication cadence and channels for high-priority stakeholders,' and 'routes users to... foundation-stakeholder-update' when you need to actually communicate. Stakeholder update analysis: 'infers the right channel and audience variant' using generic heuristics: 'engineering/design → Slack, leadership → email, mixed → Notion. This is reasonable but not universal.' → strengthens, weight 0.78: stakeholder summary's communication plan captures actual per-stakeholder channel preferences based on organizational context. This replaces stakeholder update's generic heuristic channel inference with evidence-based channel selection, improving accuracy of the output communication.

subject: skl_define-problem-statement-src-https-github-com-prod-93d7d595-skills-define-problem-statement-skill-md_93d7d595
evidence: Problem statement analysis: 'the entry point that every other skill in the suite either builds on or routes back to' and 'The output follows an external template with sections for Problem Summary, User Impact, Business Context, Success Criteria, Constraints, and Open Questions.' Stakeholder briefings analysis: 'takes any source artifact (PRD, research, GTM plan, experiment results, retro) and produces a canonical master document with numbered, traceable claims' and 'You have just finished a PRD or a major discovery synthesis. It's dense, technical, and 40 pages. Engineering needs the architecture decisions and tradeoffs. The executive sponsor needs the what, why, and investment ask in one page.' → chains_with, weight 0.85: problem statement produces a structured discovery artifact with quantified user impact, business context, and success criteria. Stakeholder briefings consumes this as a source artifact and projects audience-tailored briefings — executive gets problem summary and investment case, engineering gets constraints and success criteria, PMM gets user impact context.

subject: skl_discover-competitive-analysis-src-https-github-com-8688ef32-s-discover-competitive-analysis-skill-md_8688ef32
evidence: Competitive analysis analysis: 'routes the user to discover-market-sizing for TAM questions' and produces 'feature matrix building, 2x2 positioning mapping, and strategic recommendation generation for 3-5 competitors' including pricing comparisons and market positioning data. Market sizing analysis: uses 'comparable company' as one of four frameworks, runs 'multiple frameworks (top-down, bottom-up, comparable company, analogous market), compares where they converge and diverge,' and needs reliable competitor revenue proxies and market-share estimates. → strengthens, weight 0.72: competitive analysis output provides structured competitor intelligence (pricing, positioning, feature coverage) that market sizing's comparable-company framework needs. This reduces the agent's reliance on ad-hoc web search for competitor data during market sizing and improves the quality of revenue-proxy estimates. The chain requires moderate inference — market sizing analysis does not explicitly name competitive analysis as input, but the comparable-company framework inherently depends on the kind of competitor data competitive analysis produces.
